**RESET Statements**

> This documentation is for an unreleased version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use the latest [stable version](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/docs/sql/reference/utility/reset/>).

#  RESET Statements #

`RESET` statements are used to reset the configuration to the default.

##  Run a RESET statement #

SQL CLI

`RESET` statements can be executed in [SQL CLI](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-master/docs/sql/interfaces/sql-client/>).

The following examples show how to run a `RESET` statement in SQL CLI.

SQL CLI


    Flink SQL> RESET 'table.planner';
    [INFO] Session property has been reset.

    Flink SQL> RESET;
    [INFO] All session properties have been set to their default values.


##  Syntax #


    RESET ('key')?


If no key is specified, it reset all the properties to the default. Otherwise, reset the specified key to the default.
